rxn_class_summary: Organoboron-promoted electrochemical chlorosulfonylation of styrenes with sulfonyl chlorides via convergent paired electrolysis to give β-chlorosulfones
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of sulfonyl chloride (RSO2Cl) to sulfonyl radical + chloride; anodic oxidation of the released chloride anion to a chlorine radical, which is shuttled by p-ethylphenylboronic acid as a Cl-boron complex
electron_flow: Cathode reduces RSO2Cl to a sulfonyl radical plus chloride; the sulfonyl radical adds to the styrene giving benzylic radical P1. The chloride migrates to the anode and is oxidized to Cl•, which is captured by p-ethylphenylboronic acid; this Cl-boron complex then transfers Cl to P1 to deliver the β-chlorosulfone and a dechlorinated borane.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped from 68% to 9% (1 equiv) and n.d. (2 equiv), indicating radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product not detected with 2 equiv BHT

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signal (g=2.0056, AN=15.9 G, AH=21.7 G) for carbon radical from tosyl addition to p-F-styrene; HRMS adducts 46 (tosyl) and 47 (benzyl) detected

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl-4-methylbenzenesulfonamide
probe_reagents.observation: Chlorinated ring-closing product 48 NOT detected; instead chloro-boron complex 49 was detected by HRMS, supporting Cl• capture by the boronic acid

probe_reagents.name: divided cell (H-cell) control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, confirming necessity of paired (undivided) electrolysis

notes: CV shows chloride anion (1.42 V) is oxidized preferentially over 4-fluorostyrene (2.45 V vs 1.68 V), and TsCl shows irreversible reduction at -1.63 V; organoboron does not shift redox potentials but increases currents, supporting its role as a Cl-atom shuttle rather than redox mediator. The authors describe the process as ATRA-type within a convergent paired electrolysis framework.
